entries.description: Created ai_router/orchestrator_identity.py: shared identity resolution (effective provider by registry lookup on model -- config models keys/model_ids + normalized dot/dash token + the Copilot CLI documented model universe; provider label as explicit second choice for single-vendor engines), identityProvenance classification (direct/asserted derived from the engine, MULTI_PROVIDER_ENGINES module constant), typed fail-closed IdentityResolutionError, and the shared session-level resolver resolve_session_orchestrator_identity.

Boundary enforcement: start_session refuses a multi-provider engine without --model (exit 2, remediation names the flag) and refuses a registry-unknown model; contradicting seat labels get a stderr note (model wins at use time). Writer: session_state.build_orchestrator_block stamps identityProvenance at all three writer sites (work/typed/handoff -- L-069-1). Schema parity both directions (L-066-1): docs/session-state-schema.md 5-field block table + writer contract, dump_session_state_schema builds its example THROUGH the live writer helper, docs/session-state-schema-example.json regenerated, drift check green.

Gate consumes the helper: check_verification_integrity resolves the orchestrator side of the != check via resolve_orchestrator_identity (registry-resolved effective provider, never the free-text label); _row_provider delegates to the same shared resolve_model_provider; unresolvable identity fails closed naming start_session --model.

Dynamic verifier exclusion (F2): verify_session resolves the session orchestrator's effective provider and passes exclude_providers into routing; route(task_type=session-verification) with session context applies the identical exclusion itself via the shared resolver. pick_model/get_escalation_model treat exclusion as a hard constraint (the router-config session-verification pin demoted to a preference); copilot-cli generator-role resolution applies the exclusion against the catalog lockfile's confirmed entries; no diverse candidate raises the typed VerificationUnavailableError -- verify_session exits 7, writes no verdict, and names the operator-attested --manual-verify path.

Layer-1 pytest matrix: ai_router/tests/test_orchestrator_identity.py (67 tests) -- incident-3 regression (copilot + claude-sonnet-4.6: same-provider row refused, selection never picks anthropic under exclusion), arbitrary-label fixture (model wins over seat label), missing-model multi-provider fixture (start_session refuses; gate fails closed), single-vendor back-compat fixture (existing sets keep closing), no-diverse-catalog fixture (verification_unavailable). Updated legacy fixtures to the F1 contract (copilot invocations gain --model; block-shape assertions gain identityProvenance; the stripped-provider gate case split into fails-closed [model also stripped] and resolves-via-model). Full suite green: 2632 passed + 11 e2e passed.

Verification round 1 (gpt-5-4, selected under the new anthropic exclusion) returned ISSUES_FOUND with 3 Major findings; all three accepted and fixed. I-084-S1-1: shipped the machine-readable ai_router/schemas/session-state.schema.json (the spec's named Touches surface) + TestSessionStateSchemaParity (writer output validates; the schema rejects bad identityProvenance tokens; plan-less carve-out and pre-084 blocks accepted) and pointed docs/session-state-schema.md at it. I-084-S1-2: verify_session now imports VerificationUnavailableError package-relative first so the except clause catches the exact class route() raises (the distinct-class miss reproduced live in tests before the fix). I-084-S1-3: route() now ALWAYS resolves the session-derived exclusion for session-verification with session context and UNIONS it with any caller-supplied list (a bare caller can add exclusions, never remove the orchestrator's). Full suite green: 2642 passed, 5 skipped.

Verification round 2 (gpt-5-4 under exclusion) confirmed the R1 fixes and found 1 NEW Major (I-084-S1-4): start_session only validated supplied models for multi-provider engines, so a typoed model on a single-vendor engine could persist and silently defer identity to the free-text label. Accepted and fixed: the boundary now refuses ANY supplied model that does not resolve (every engine); registry coverage added for current orchestrator-only models via disabled (is_enabled: false) router-config entries (claude-sonnet-5, gemini-3-pro) plus dated-snapshot-id normalization (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 -> claude-haiku-4-5); the read-side single-vendor fallback is retained ONLY as legacy tolerance for pre-084 files (documented residual — this repo's history carries claude-sonnet-5 and dated ids) since the boundary prevents new occurrences. Cross-round ledger: I-084-S1-1..3 RESOLVED (no resurrections), I-084-S1-4 fixed. Suite green: 2646 passed, 5 skipped.

Verification round 3 (gpt-5-4 under exclusion) confirmed rounds 1-2 fixes (no resurrections) and found 2 NEW Majors, both accepted and fixed. I-084-S1-5: the dated-snapshot normalization was unscoped, so an INVENTED dated variant of any provider's id (gpt-5.4-20251001) normalized onto a real registry entry and slipped the boundary -- the -YYYYMMDD strip is now scoped to the claude-* family (Anthropic's dated-alias convention); invented dated variants of other providers now fail to resolve and are refused. I-084-S1-6: under copilot-cli the exclusion was judged against the generator prefer list only, so verification_unavailable could fire while the seat catalog held a confirmed different-provider model outside prefer -- _resolve_copilot_generator now falls back to scanning the FULL confirmed catalog (provider allowed + not excluded) when an exclusion is active; verification_unavailable fires only when the confirmed catalog truly has no diverse candidate; the no-exclusion pre-084 prefer-only contract is unchanged. Suite green: 2653 passed, 5 skipped. Ledger: I-084-S1-1..4 RESOLVED, -5/-6 fixed.

Verification round 4 (gpt-5-4 under the anthropic exclusion): VERIFIED, 0 findings, 0 nits. The dogfood premise held every round: the verifier was selected by the new dynamic exclusion (orchestrator claude-fable-5 resolved to anthropic via the model registry, anthropic excluded by resolution). Four rounds total, six Major findings found and fixed (I-084-S1-1..6), zero resurrections. Routed verification cost: R1 $0.28 + R2 $0.42 + R3 $0.37 + R4 $0.39 = $1.46.

F3 row stamping: new ai_router/verification_stamp.py (sanctioned sources, code-minted versioned template id session-verification-v1, normalized template hash, evidence hash, byte-exact artifact binding, package version; producer build_stamp + route-time complete_stamp + consumer validate_stamped_row). record_call writes the nine additive stamp fields (null on historical rows); route() completes and records the stamp on both the api and copilot-cli paths (ValueError outside session-verification); verify_session builds and passes the stamp and writes artifacts newline='' so on-disk bytes equal the stamped hash.

Gate tightening: check_verification_integrity's api arm now accepts ONLY rows with a valid, internally consistent stamp (shared find_session_verification_evidence path; template-hash mismatch, missing fields, edited artifacts, copied stamps, same-provider verifiers all fail closed with named reasons). Bare route() rows no longer corroborate; refusals keep naming the venv-qualified sanctioned command.

The close backstop: new ai_router/close_backstop.py wired into close_session.run before the gate chain. On a Full-tier close without settling stamped evidence the framework runs the verification itself in-process through the S1 F1/F2 machinery (same evidence assembly with a pre-session diff base resolved from startedAt, same canonical template, same exclusion, stamped row + raw artifacts + disposition patch), then proceeds on VERIFIED / refuses with findings on blocking ISSUES_FOUND / blocks explicitly on verification_unavailable or double transport failure (never a pass). Respects budget.yaml zero-budget passthrough, the two-attempt ladder, the close lock (idempotent); --manual-verify stays the attested bypass; --force triggers no metered call and still cannot pass unverified. Backstop-written artifacts are close-out bookkeeping the working-tree gate tolerates for that one close (session-events.jsonl precedent).

Layer-1 matrix: test_close_backstop.py (20 tests: incident-1 self-attested close -- backstop verdict governs both ways; incident-2 null-verdict close verified-or-blocked; incident-3 bare-route row rejected + backstop runs with anthropic excluded; verification_unavailable blocks naming --manual-verify; two-attempt ladder; zero-budget passthrough; force/manual-verify neither triggers the backstop; Minor-only settled claims stand it down; idempotent re-close; pre-session diff base), test_verification_stamp.py (12 producer-side unit tests), 11 new stamp-layer gate tests (template-hash mismatch, per-field missing-stamp matrix, edited artifact, copied stamp, exclusion mismatch, backstop source). Existing close-path fixtures upgraded to stamped evidence via shared tests/stamp_fixtures.py; conftest autouse guard refuses live routed calls from the backstop in tests. Fixed a live I-084-S1-2-class defect: the backstop catches VerificationUnavailableError under both module identities. Full suite: 2713 passed, 5 skipped; e2e: 11 passed.

Dogfood round 1 (LIVE): close_session invoked with no manual verify_session; the backstop ran the verification in-process (verifier gpt-5-4, anthropic excluded by resolution, diff base = last pre-session commit 4649a63, $0.2537) and REFUSED the close with 3 Major findings -- the live proof the framework holds the last word. All three accepted and remediated (ledger, zero resurrections): I-084-S2-1 --force no longer skips the backstop (an unverified force-close gets the same in-process verification; incident recovery under a dead provider is the attested --manual-verify path); I-084-S2-2 TEMPLATE_HASHES pins each template id to an immutable normalized hash, enforced at stamp production AND consumption, plus a tripwire test that fails on any unbumped template edit; I-084-S2-3 the disposition/ledger dogfood narrative rewritten to stay within its evidence (L-073-1). Re-close lets the backstop run round 2 live; its verdict governs.

Dogfood round 2 (LIVE, $0.3509, gpt-5-4): refused again with 2 Major findings; both trace to one genuine class omission, fixed as I-084-S2-4: the backstop sent no up-front conventions block (the promoted L-064-10 Convention). Without it a mid-close verifier structurally reports 'the close is not finished' (the evidence cannot contain closeout_succeeded during the verification that gates it) and re-litigates the severity-derived blocking predicate (L-071-1, preserved by the spec's non-goals; Minor-only proceeds). run_close_backstop now builds a built-in conventions preamble (mid-close view, L-071-1 discipline, narrow re-verify rounds) in the Original Task slot; the canonical template is untouched and the F3 template binding unchanged; close-out.md grounds the Minor-only-proceeds contract in L-071-1. Ledger: no resurrections -- I-084-S2-1..3 fixes stand; round-2 Issue 1 is the structural mid-flight view (context, not defect) and Issue 2 the bare-token reading (context), both addressed by giving the verifier the context. Round 3 decides.

Dogfood round 3 (LIVE, $0.4213, gpt-5-4, conventions block in play): the mid-flight false positives are gone; two NEW genuine Majors found and fixed (ledger, zero resurrections). I-084-S2-5: stale stamped evidence could settle a later close -- the stamp now carries evidence_base (resolved diff-base sha) + work_diff_sha256 (a canonical-exclusion work-diff hash; producers compute it, validate_stamped_row recomputes it against the tree being closed, so any post-verification work change makes the row stale and the backstop runs a fresh round; one shared path covers the gate, the backstop skip-check, and the fixtures). I-084-S2-6: the fresh-repo / missing-startedAt fallback silently degraded evidence to a HEAD diff -- no pre-session commit now diffs from git's empty tree, and missing startedAt fails closed naming verify_session --diff-base. Nit accepted: earlier ledger entries' test counts are superseded -- test_close_backstop.py now carries 25 tests; suite 2717+ passed. Round 4 decides.

Dogfood round 4 (LIVE, $0.5014, gpt-5-4): two NEW Majors, both fixed. I-084-S2-7: the stamp now carries the VERDICT parsed at record time from the artifact-bound bytes; the close gate and the backstop skip predicate both require the disposition's claim to equal a stamped verdict -- a hand-flipped VERIFIED cannot ride an ISSUES_FOUND row. I-084-S2-5 (refinement; previously incomplete): freshness exclusions narrowed from whole-set-dir + docs/planning to the exact bookkeeping files, and the freshness primitive became a content DIGEST (path + blob hash of all files differing from base, tracked or untracked) -- stable across the untracked-to-tracked commit transition (L-064-9's diff-invisible new deliverables) while any content change stales the row. Lock files and the workflow-generated change-log.md classified as bookkeeping. Both nits fixed. Suite 2724 passed. Round 5 decides.
